text: This is Dr. Craig Rogers, Director of Renal Surgery at the Vatakuta Urology Institute at Henry Ford Hospital. In this video, we show kidney mobilization and exposing the tumor for optimal resection.
The surgeon uses the fourth robotic arm to adjust the position of the kidney, enhancing exposure of the tumor for resection.

The surgeon incises the Girotis fascia near the tumor, avoiding direct incision over the tumor itself. The exposure begins over the normal capsule, progressing inward to reveal the tumor while carefully preserving its integrity.

The surgeon mobilizes the kidney to achieve a direct view of the tumor, ensuring at least two centimeters of normal surrounding capsule are visible for adequate resection margins and placement of capsular sutures.
The surgeon uses the prograsp and the fourth arm to position the kidney, repeating the steps to expose the tumor located at the lower pole for optimal resection.
In the next video, we demonstrate the use of intraoperative ultrasound to help define the tumor for resection.
